calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Initial Responses Of Defendant Institute On Aging To Special Interrogatories, Set One; Form Interrogatories (General), Set One; And Form Interrogatories (Employment), Set One
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Joseph Tojarieh, Esq. 310-553-5539 Stone Brook Law for Plaintiff.
Tracy Mainguy, Esq. 415-926-7600 Kaufman Dolowich for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.
The Court inquires on the proposed orders. Ms. Mainguy states they have been having technical difficulties.
The Court adminishes parties; level of professionalism, no regard to rules, lack of respect, comply with 2.7B, coordination, personal conduct, sandbagging, learn local rules, will reconsider this motion and will impose significant amount of sanctions if this comes back, Defense' responses to Plaintiff by the end of the month,
Mr. Tojarieh states timeline, no responses in 5 months.
Ms. Mainguy responds; technical difficulties, apologizes to the Court.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff Jamela De Leon's Motion To Compel Initial Responses Of Defendant Institute On Aging To Special Interrogatories, Set One; Form Interrogatories (General), Set One; And Form Interrogatories (Employment), Set One.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***
